prerequisites: Check available disk space: dir bootflash: (NX-OS/IOS-XE) or show media (IOS-XR)
Backup running configuration to external server
Verify new image integrity (MD5/SHA512 checksum against cisco.com)
Review release notes for target version — check for known caveats and ISSU support
Confirm hardware compatibility (image must support installed line cards / supervisors)
Schedule maintenance window with change control approval
Ensure console access is available (not just SSH/VTY)
For dual-sup systems: verify standby sup is online and in sync
purpose: Upgrade, downgrade, or patch the OS image on a network device [nxos — method: install all (ISSU or disruptive); platforms: Nexus 9000, Nexus 3000, Nexus 7000]

rollback.action: If device fails to boot: from loader prompt, use 'boot nxos bootflash:<previous_image>'
If ISSU fails mid-process: system automatically rolls back to previous image

steps.notes: Use SCP, SFTP, or USB. Verify MD5 after copy: show file bootflash:<image> md5sum

commands.command: show install all impact nxos bootflash:<image>

steps.notes: Shows whether ISSU or disruptive upgrade will occur. Look for 'non-disruptive' in output. If not supported, plan for disruptive reload.
This is the preferred method. Handles boot variable, compatibility checks, and ISSU if supported. Do NOT use 'boot nxos' unless directed by TAC.
Watch for 'Install has been successful' or error messages. On ISSU: supervisor failover occurs mid-process — do not interrupt.
Confirm new version is running. Also check: show module, show vpc (if applicable), show ip route summary, show bgp ipv4 unicast summary.

verification.confirms_if: Boot variable points to new image (survives reload)
All line cards and supervisors are operational

warnings: ISSU is NOT supported on all platforms or between all version combinations — always verify the ISSU compatibility matrix
Always have console access before starting an upgrade — if the device fails to boot you need console to recover
Verify boot variables after upgrade to prevent boot loop on next reload
On dual-sup systems, upgrade BOTH supervisors — mismatched versions cause unpredictable behavior
SMU/GISO patches may have dependencies — install in the order specified by release notes
Never delete the currently running image until the new image is confirmed stable
On vPC domains: upgrade one peer at a time, verify vPC is stable between upgrades
NX-OS requires 'feature' re-enablement after some major version jumps — check release notes
Compact images (compact-) are smaller but don't support ISSU
